children.label: Specify a path to a virtual environment (note: optional, provide a path on the filesystem if you want to persist your virtual environment):
Install system site packages (inherited from global environment)
Enter the path to your requirements.txt file (or simply list your packages with a space between)

children.text:
Notes: 
1. Creation of a virtual environment also activates the same.
2. The virtual environment will be created under a folder named venv.

Python - Create a Virtual Environment
===============================

The "Python - Create a Virtual Environment" custom step helps users create a virtual Python environment, install a given set of packages if needed, and work within the confines of the new Python environment for project or program-specific purposes.

Users may choose to persist the virtual environment folder for later reuse, by simply providing a full path to a persistent location folder. 

For more details, refer https://blogs.sas.com/content/subconsciousmusings/2022/05/16/python-a-la-carte/

1. Path to a virtual environment (optional):specify a path located on a shared file system, which you expect to access through another SAS session later on.  Most SAS Viya deployments may contain a /mnt/... folder location which serves this purpose.

2. Requirements or a path to a requirements file (optional): Users also have an option to provide a requirements file consisting of packages which they would like pip-installed.  Either provide a path to a requirements.txt file which contains a list of python packages (and optionally, specific versions), or provide a list of packages as a string, separated by a space.

3. Install system site packages (checkbox): Users may choose to inherit the global environment's site packages into the virtual environment, by checking this option.
Sundaresh Sankaran (sundaresh.sankaran@sas.com / sundaresh.sankaran@gmail.com) and Ali Aiello (ali.aiello@sas.com)
